libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are needed for meaningful coverage. Generated tests should avoid the removed TypedActor APIs and instead exercise remaining pekko-actor classic APIs such as ActorSystem, Props, Actor/AbstractActor, actor creation, message dispatch, scheduler, DynamicAccess/ReflectiveDynamicAccess, serialization, and coordinated shutdown. Do not add pekko-actor-typed unless intentionally testing the separate typed-actor module rather than this pekko-actor artifact.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8742 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Pekko 2.0.0-M1 is an experimental milestone with Java 17+ minimum support and many deprecated APIs removed, so tests copied from 1.1.5 may compile-fail even without setup problems.

Documentation for the new typed Actor API uses org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor-typed_2.13, but adding that dependency would broaden coverage to a different module and is not required to cover pekko-actor_2.13 itself.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No concrete extra setup is required. The resolved org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1 artifact depends only on scala-library 2.13.18 and com.typesafe:config 1.4.5, contains the classic org.apache.pekko.actor APIs, and does not require Docker or external services. The reported failure is caused by API drift: deprecated classic TypedActor/TypedProps/TypedActorExtension classes present in 1.1.5 are absent in 2.0.0-M1.
